is_p_falciparum.evidence: Patients with uncomplicated falciparum malaria; 'frequent exposure to Plasmodium falciparum'; markers in pfcrt, dhfr, dhps genes of P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: pfcrt Thr-76 (codon 76), dhfr Ile-51 (51), Asn-108 (108), dhps Gly-437 (437), Glu-540 (540) — all curated target codons; also dhfr 59 (not curated) and dhfr 164 (not curated).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 2 gives no. (%) of patients wild type/mixed/pure mutant per site for each mutation, e.g. dhps Gly-437 pure mutant Kanungu 274 (78).

sub_country_location.evidence: Table 2 breaks down frequencies by 6 named sites: Kanungu, Mubende, Jinja, Arua, Tororo, Apac — all sub-national Ugandan locations shown on map (Figure 1).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: 'conducted between December 2002 and May 2004' — collection window under 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Pretreatment samples from patients with uncomplicated malaria enrolled in clinical trials at 6 sites in Uganda; blood collected on filter paper on day of diagnosis.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study analyzing genotypes from 2084 patients via nested PCR-RFLP.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples were from previously published antimalarial drug-efficacy studies [5,6] (Bakyaita et al 2005; Yeka et al 2005) at the same sites; also '[18]' Talisuna compared the same sites. Overlap with those prior collections is plausible.

supplement.evidence: All per-locus frequency counts and site-level breakdowns are in Table 2 of the main text; no supplementary data required.

assessment.summary: Cross-sectional molecular analysis of pretreatment isolates from 6 sites in Uganda (Dec 2002–May 2004) reporting per-site frequencies of pfcrt 76, dhfr 51/59/108/164, dhps 437/540 mutations. Table 2 provides extractable numerator/denominator counts per site; temporal window under 3 years. Meets all criteria; medium duplicate risk since samples derive from prior published efficacy trials at the same sites.
